atoms.source_snippet: pub fn add_fulfilled_async(&mut self, tx_hash, tx_for_execution, tx_size, effective_gas, settlement_tx, settlement_size, precompile_address, input, commit_block, execution_result, execution_proof, base_fee) { ... self.fulfilled_count += 1; }
atoms.semantic_claim: Fulfilled async entries are prepared as an atomic original-plus-settlement bundle carrying precompile input, commit block, execution result, and proof before tip sorting and execution.

destinations.evidence: Destination prepares fulfilled async settlement state, validates proof/result, constructs the settlement transaction, and annotates the original transaction outside a unified enum.

destinations.snippet:
Err(reason) => { ... "skipping fulfilled async settlement entry" ... }
"atomicity violation prevented: settlement skipped after fulfilled original execution"
destinations.evidence: Destination executes the prepared original and settlement sequentially and treats a skipped settlement after original execution as fatal.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Destination covers the fulfilled async preparation and atomic settlement requirement with stricter validation and explicit fatal atomicity handling, but it does not place the pair in a global tip-sorted prepared bundle.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Force a fulfilled async original to execute while its settlement would exceed gas, and assert destination aborts the payload attempt with the atomicity violation instead of committing only the original.
atoms.notes: Confirmed covered: fulfilled async preparation keeps original transaction, settlement transaction, proof/result metadata, RLP lengths, and atomic execution semantics.

atoms.source_snippet: pub fn add_delivery(&mut self, origin_tx_hash: B256, settlement_tx: Recovered<TransactionSigned>, effective_tip: u128) { let bundle = PreparedBundle::DeliverySettlement { origin_tx_hash, settlement_tx, effective_tip }; ... self.delivery_count += 1; }
atoms.semantic_claim: Delivery-ready async work is represented as a settlement-only bundle keyed by origin transaction hash and carrying an explicit effective tip for ordering.

destinations.evidence: Destination constructs delivery settlements from the original transaction hash and delivery fee settings, but executes them in the delivery-ready stage rather than adding them to a global effective-tip bundle.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Destination preserves delivery settlement identity and fee derivation, but the source's explicit `effective_tip` ordering field is not used for cross-source ordering.

atoms.semantic_claim: System settlement gas accounting uses calldata-based gas instead of raw `u64::MAX` gas limits, with a 500,000 gas fallback estimate on calculation failure.

destinations.snippet:
let settlement_calldata_gas = calculate_calldata_gas(settlement_tx.input()).map_err(|err| format!("settlement calldata gas calculation failed: {err}"))?;
let calldata_gas = match calculate_calldata_gas(settlement_tx.input()) { Ok(gas) => gas, Err(err) => return Err(BlockExecutionError::msg(format!("Gas calculation overflow: {err}"))) };
destinations.evidence: Destination payload code uses calldata gas for async settlements and fails on overflow rather than falling back to 500,000.

destinations.snippet: if tx.tx().is_type(ASYNC_SETTLEMENT_TX_TYPE_ID) { Some(calculate_calldata_gas(tx.tx().input()).map_err(BlockExecutionError::msg)?) } else { None }
destinations.evidence: The EVM dependency applies a calldata-gas block override for async settlement transaction results.

destinations.snippet:
phase1_expected_gas = calculate_calldata_gas(phase1_input)
verify_tx_gas_complete(... expected_gas=phase1_expected_gas, expected_gas_type="calldata", ...)
destinations.evidence: E2E tests assert calldata-based gas for settlement transactions.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The consensus-relevant calldata-based settlement gas rule moved into destination payload and Alloy EVM code; the old 500,000 fallback is intentionally not preserved because destination treats gas calculation overflow as an error/skip path.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Create an async settlement with very large calldata that overflows gas calculation and assert destination rejects or skips it rather than using a silent 500,000 fallback.
atoms.notes: Confirmed covered with current stronger fail-closed accounting: settlement calldata gas is computed and post-original settlement skips are fatal for fulfilled async atomicity.

atoms.semantic_claim: When enabled, payload sequencing-rights classification simulates transactions with `ContractCallInspector`; if simulation fails the transaction is classified as no sequencing rights, and if the build deadline is exceeded after classification the entire reorder is skipped.

destinations.snippet: let classification = try_classify_static(&candidate.tx).unwrap_or_else(|| { classify_top_level_with_sr(&candidate.tx, &reader, state.as_ref()) });
destinations.evidence: Payload-local sequencing-rights code uses top-level classification only and has no deadline parameter or EVM simulation branch.

destinations.snippet:
pub struct ContractCallInspector { ... }
pub fn sequencing_rights_full_call_graph_enabled() -> bool { std::env::var(SR_FULL_CALL_GRAPH_ENABLED_ENV).map(|v| v == "true" || v == "1").unwrap_or(false) }
destinations.evidence: The dependency still contains the inspector and environment gate, but the destination payload path does not wire them into candidate ordering.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Enable `RITUAL_SEQUENCING_RIGHTS_FULL_CALL_GRAPH_ENABLED=true` and use a transaction whose sequencing-rights contract call is only visible through an internal call; verify destination payload ordering does not change via full-call-graph classification.
atoms.notes: Confirmed missing: full-call-graph sequencing classification through ContractCallInspector is not wired; current deadline handling can skip reorder, but only after static/top-level classification support.

atoms.semantic_claim: After classification, the file calls `reorder_block`, skips reordering on errors or count mismatches, and maps reordered transaction hashes back to prepared entries for final bundle ordering.

destinations.evidence: Payload sequencing-rights classification is wired for static and top-level classification over PayloadCandidate::primary_tx and fails open to the existing order on state/read/reorder errors.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The reorder/error/hash-remap behavior is structurally equivalent for regular pool candidates, but source applied it to every prepared bundle.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Make `reorder_block` return a hash sequence with a missing transaction and assert destination preserves original candidate order rather than dropping or duplicating entries.
atoms.notes: Confirmed covered: reorder_block failures/count mismatches preserve existing order and successful reordered hashes are mapped back to candidates.

atoms.source_snippet:
pub struct UnifiedExecutionResult {
    pub cumulative_gas_used: u64,
    pub total_fees: U256,
    pub current_block_size: usize,
    pub blob_count: u64,
    pub scheduled_executed: usize,
    pub fulfilled_executed: usize,
    pub delivery_executed: usize,
    pub regular_executed: usize,
}
atoms.semantic_claim: Unified execution returns cumulative gas, total fees, block size, blob count, and per-source executed counts for scheduled, fulfilled async, delivery, and regular transactions.

destinations.evidence: Destination keeps gas, fee, size, and blob counters as local builder state and emits total fees in the built payload; it does not expose per-source execution counts.
Async settlement execution updates destination gas and fee counters through a shared helper.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Destination preserves consensus-sensitive accounting through local payload state and helpers, but drops the public unified result struct and per-source executed counters.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Execute a mixed payload with scheduled, fulfilled async, delivery settlement, and regular transactions, then assert cumulative gas, blob count, total fees, and RLP size match expected staged accounting.
atoms.notes: Confirmed covered by current PayloadCandidate/ritual_async.rs behavior for fulfilled async, delivery, regular, and pooled scheduled candidates; scheduler-state obligations remain staged ahead of the candidate list and per-source counters/log metrics are support-only.

atoms.semantic_claim: The file adds a unit test asserting default unified execution results start with zero cumulative gas and zero total fees.

destinations.snippet:
#[test]
fn permutation_maps_cba_tip_order_to_abc_priority_order() { ... }
destinations.evidence: Destination has adapted tests for payload-local sequencing behavior rather than a unified result default test.
Destination tests cover replacement async delivery fee/config behavior but not the removed default result struct.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The trivial default-result assertion is obsolete with destination local accounting; stronger adapted tests cover fee/config and sequencing helpers.
atoms.notes: Confirmed support-only absence: old default UnifiedExecutionResult unit test is tied to an absent helper struct and is not a runtime behavior gap.

summary: Mechanical review complete. Current PayloadCandidate and ritual_async.rs cover most of the old unified_execution.rs runtime behavior: common candidate identity, primary transactions for sequencing, fulfilled async pair atomicity, delivery effective tips, candidate sorting, gas/size accounting, and fail-open reorder. Confirmed remaining gaps are scheduler-state obligations outside the candidate list, missing full-call-graph sequencing classification, and support-only metrics/per-source counters.
notes: Reviewed against current port after PayloadCandidate landed; no atoms remain in needs-review state.
